revision.comment: 新しいページ：大園古墳（大阪府高石市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 大園古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
大園古墳跡 説明板 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[高石市]]西取石8丁目 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 56.85 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 25.87 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長53m（または57m） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = |ラベル = 大園古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''大園古墳'''（おおぞのこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]西取石にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+和泉北部の首長墓系譜「和泉北部の首長系譜」『和泉市の考古・古代・中世 （和泉市の歴史6）』 和泉市史編さん委員会編、和泉市・ぎょうせい、2013年、pp. 65-70。 !colspan=2|群!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期 |- |北|| ||[[和泉黄金塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||94m||4c末 |- | ||南||丸笠山古墳||前方後円墳||96m||4c末 |- | ||南||鍋塚古墳||[[円墳]]||60-70m||5c初 |- | ||南||王塚古墳||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||46m||5c中 |- |北|| ||[[信太貝吹山古墳]]||帆立貝形古墳||60m||5c前 |- |北|| ||カニヤ塚古墳||帆立貝形古墳||不明||5c後 |- |北|| ||'''大園古墳'''||帆立貝形古墳||50m||5c末 |- |北|| ||[[富木車塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||48m||6c中 |- | ||南||狐塚古墳||前方後円墳||58m||6c後 |}
大阪府南部、信太山丘陵北西麓の低位段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[1974年|1974]]・[[1976年]]（[[昭和]]49・51年）に大阪府教育委員会・高石市教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されたのち、現在は消滅している{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。調査時点では既に墳丘は完全に削平されていたが、墳丘長は約53メートル{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または約57メートル史跡説明板。）、後円部は直径約34メートル、前方部は幅約25メートルを測った{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面には[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が存在したと見られ、いずれも周濠内に転落したものが検出されている{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。特に埴輪には[[円筒埴輪]]のほか、多数の[[形象埴輪]]（家形・人物形・馬形・鶏形・草摺形・盾形・靫形・蓋形・剣形埴輪）が認められている{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲には幅3-4メートルの周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は削平のため明らかでない。 この大園古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。本古墳を含む一帯の大園遺跡は古墳時代の大規模集落（[[掘立柱建物]]群）として知られ、本古墳はその首長墓に位置づけられる。また和泉地方北部では首長墓古墳として北群・南群の2群が認められるが、北群・南群にはそれぞれ時期的空白が認められることから両群が輪番制で首長を担ったと推定され、本古墳はそのうちの1つに位置づけられる『大園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -土地区画整理事業に伴う葛の葉地区の調査-』 和泉市教育委員会、2014年、pp. 1-4。。 ファイル:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|埴輪 鶏
{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（高石市、1994年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=大園遺跡|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|大園遺跡（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=松村隆文|year=1989|chapter=大園古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} {{デフォルトソート:おおそのこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 大園古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
大園古墳跡 説明板 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[高石市]]西取石8丁目 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 56.85 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 25.87 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長53m（または57m） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = |ラベル = 大園古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''大園古墳'''（おおぞのこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]西取石にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+和泉北部の首長墓系譜「和泉北部の首長系譜」『和泉市の考古・古代・中世 （和泉市の歴史6）』 和泉市史編さん委員会編、和泉市・ぎょうせい、2013年、pp. 65-70。 !colspan=2|群!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期 |- |北|| ||[[和泉黄金塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||94m||4c末 |- | ||南||丸笠山古墳||前方後円墳||96m||4c末 |- | ||南||鍋塚古墳||[[円墳]]||60-70m||5c初 |- | ||南||王塚古墳||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||46m||5c中 |- |北|| ||[[信太貝吹山古墳]]||帆立貝形古墳||60m||5c前 |- |北|| ||カニヤ塚古墳||帆立貝形古墳||不明||5c後 |- |北|| ||'''大園古墳'''||帆立貝形古墳||50m||5c末 |- |北|| ||[[富木車塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||48m||6c中 |- | ||南||狐塚古墳||前方後円墳||58m||6c後 |}
大阪府南部、信太山丘陵北西麓の低位段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[1974年|1974]]・[[1976年]]（[[昭和]]49・51年）に大阪府教育委員会・高石市教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されたのち、現在は消滅している{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。調査時点では既に墳丘は完全に削平されていたが、墳丘長は約53メートル{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または約57メートル史跡説明板。）、後円部は直径約34メートル、前方部は幅約25メートルを測った{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面には[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が存在したと見られ、いずれも周濠内に転落したものが検出されている{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。特に埴輪には[[円筒埴輪]]のほか、多数の[[形象埴輪]]（家形・人物形・馬形・鶏形・草摺形・盾形・靫形・蓋形・剣形埴輪）が認められている{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲には幅3-4メートルの周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。主体部の埋葬施設は削平のため明らかでない。 この大園古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。本古墳を含む一帯の大園遺跡は古墳時代の大規模集落（[[掘立柱建物]]群）として知られ、本古墳はその首長墓に位置づけられる。また和泉地方北部では首長墓古墳として北群・南群の2群が認められるが、北群・南群にはそれぞれ時期的空白が認められることから両群が輪番制で首長を担ったと推定され、本古墳はそのうちの1つに位置づけられる『大園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -土地区画整理事業に伴う葛の葉地区の調査-』 和泉市教育委員会、2014年、pp. 1-4。。 ファイル:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|埴輪 鶏

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb Name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

(Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Hotategaigata Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Hotategaigata Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Hotategaigata Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Hotategaigata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Informational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Informational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Informational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite
