{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Informational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Informational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! Size !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | Group !! Tomb name !! Shape !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Informational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial_chamber = Unknown | artifacts = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | designation = Not designated }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} shape. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. Among the haniwa, various shapes were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-typed, human-typed, horse-typed, chicken-typed, grass skirt-typed, shield-typed, quiver-typed, lid-typed, and sword-typed varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its dimensions were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersInformational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | other_designation = Not other_designation1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | other_designation = Not other_designation1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | other_designation = Not other_designation1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! Period |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | other_designation = Not other_designation1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4.
