Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6|12=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ozono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takaishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Ozono Kofun]] [[ja:大園古墳]]

The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6|12=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ozono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takaishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Ozono Kofun]] [[ja:大園古墳]]

Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ozono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takaishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ozono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ozono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ozono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ozono Kofun]] [[ja:大園古墳]]

revision.comment: 3 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大園古墳 up to rev 91584117
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 大園古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
大園古墳跡 説明板 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[高石市]]西取石8丁目 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 56.85 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 25.87 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長53m（または57m） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = |ラベル = 大園古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''大園古墳'''（おおぞのこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]西取石にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+和泉北部の首長墓系譜「和泉北部の首長系譜」『和泉市の考古・古代・中世 （和泉市の歴史6）』 和泉市史編さん委員会編、和泉市・ぎょうせい、2013年、pp. 65-70。 !colspan=2|群!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期 |- |北|| ||[[和泉黄金塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||94m||4c末 |- | ||南||丸笠山古墳||前方後円墳||96m||4c末 |- | ||南||鍋塚古墳||[[円墳]]||60-70m||5c初 |- | ||南||王塚古墳||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||46m||5c中 |- |北|| ||[[信太貝吹山古墳]]||帆立貝形古墳||60m||5c前 |- |北|| ||カニヤ塚古墳||帆立貝形古墳||不明||5c後 |- |北|| ||'''大園古墳'''||帆立貝形古墳||50m||5c末 |- |北|| ||[[富木車塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||48m||6c中 |- | ||南||狐塚古墳||前方後円墳||58m||6c後 |}
大阪府南部、信太山丘陵北西麓の低位段丘上に築造された古墳である。[[1974年|1974]]・[[1976年]]（[[昭和]]49・51年）に大阪府教育委員会・高石市教育委員会による発掘調査が実施されたのち、現在は消滅している{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。調査時点では既に墳丘は完全に削平されていたが、墳丘長は約53メートル{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}（または約57メートル史跡説明板。）、後円部は直径約34メートル、前方部は幅約25メートルを測った{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘表面には[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]が存在したと見られ、いずれも周濠内に転落したものが検出されている{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。特に埴輪には[[円筒埴輪]]のほか、多数の[[形象埴輪]]（家形・人物形・馬形・鶏形・草摺形・盾形・靫形・蓋形・剣形埴輪）が認められている{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲には幅3-4メートルの周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は削平のため明らかでない。 この大園古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}。本古墳を含む一帯の大園遺跡は古墳時代の大規模集落（[[掘立柱建物]]群）として知られ、本古墳はその首長墓に位置づけられる。また和泉地方北部では首長墓古墳として北群・南群の2群が認められるが、北群・南群にはそれぞれ時期的空白が認められることから両群が輪番制で首長を担ったと推定され、本古墳はそのうちの1つに位置づけられる『大園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -土地区画整理事業に伴う葛の葉地区の調査-』 和泉市教育委員会、2014年、pp. 1-4。。 ファイル:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|埴輪 鶏
{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（高石市、1994年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=大園遺跡|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|大園遺跡（平凡社）|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=松村隆文|year=1989|chapter=大園古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|大園古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} {{デフォルトソート:おおそのこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大園古墳|version=91584117|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ozono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takaishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ozono Kofun]] [[ja:大園古墳]]

The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qq=}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大園古墳|version=91584117|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ozono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takaishi, Osaka]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ozono Kofun]] [[ja:大園古墳]]

The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|56.85|N|135|26|25.87|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} | period = Late [[5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|ja|高石市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉市の歴史6|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|qq=}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|lt=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|ja|和泉黄金塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} || Scallop Kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || Scallop Kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || Scallop Kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|qq=}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} and {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[5th century]] during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|qq=}}.}} == (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of
