This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.515792|N|135.440519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | period = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉の Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || [[:en:Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun]] || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || scallop-shaped kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} || scallop-shaped kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || scallop-shaped kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || scallop-shaped kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信太山演習場}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] and [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. [[ja:大園古墳]] [[ja:大園古墳]] [[ja:大園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55535989]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ōzono Kofun (大園古墳) | image = [[File:Ozono Kofun, setsumeiban.jpg|280px]]
Intypeational sign at the site of Ōzono Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.515792|N|135.440519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 53m (or 57m) | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} | period = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} | Historic Site = Not Historic Site1 | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Ōzono Kofun''' (大園古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉の Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! colspan="2" | part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Marukasa-yama Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 96m || Late 4th century |- | || South || Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | || South || Ōtsuka Kofun || scallop-shaped kofun || 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} || scallop-shaped kofun || 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || scallop-shaped kofun || Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || scallop-shaped kofun || 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} || Zenpokoenfun || 48m || Mid-6th century |- | || South || Kitsunezuka Kofun || Zenpokoenfun || 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信太山演習場}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信太山演習場}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. The mound is no longer extant. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Lineage of Izumi Northern Chief Tombs"Lineage of Izumi Northern Chiefs," in {{ill|Izumi City History Volume 6|ja|和泉の Municipal History6|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi City History Volume 6|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval, Izumi City Editorial Committee, Izumi City, Gyosei, 2013, pp. 65–70. ! part_of !! Tomb name !! type !! area !! built |- | North || {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} || 94m || Late 4th century |- | South|| Marukasa-yama Kofun || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}|| 96m || Late 4th century |- | South|| Nabetsuka Kofun || {{ill|Empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 60–70m || Early 5th century |- | South|| Ōtsuka Kofun || {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|| 46m || Mid-5th century |- | North || {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|| 60m || Early 5th century |- | North || Kaniya-zuka Kofun || {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|| Unknown || Late 5th century |- | North || '''Ōzono Kofun''' || {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|| 50m || Late 5th century |- | North || {{ill|Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|富木車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tonoki Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}|| 48m || Mid-6th century |- | South|| Kitsunezuka Kofun || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}|| 58m || Late 6th century |}
Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信太山演習場}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大園古墳|version=91584117|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大園古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55535989}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaishi]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信太山演習場}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. Ōzono Kofun was located on the northwestern terrace of the {{ill|Shinodayama Hills|ja|信太山丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinodayama Hills|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信太山演習場}} in southern Osaka. The site was excavated in 1974 and 1976 by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education and Takaishi City Board of Education. The mound was subsequently destroyed, leaving no visible remains.Matsumura, Takafumi. "Ōzono Kofun," in {{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989. The tumulus had a short-fronted {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type. While the mound was already flattened by the time of excavation, its area were estimated as follows: total length approximately 53 meters (or possibly 57 metersIntypeational sign.), rear circular section diameter about 34 meters, and front section width about 25 meters. The surface of the mound featured {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, some of which were recovered from the surrounding moat. Among the haniwa, various types were identified, including cylindrical, house-shaped, human-shaped, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, grass skirt-shaped, shield-shaped, quiver-shaped, lid-shaped, and sword-shaped varieties. The moat surrounding the mound was approximately 3–4 meters wide. The burial chamber remains unknown due to the mound's destruction. Ōzono Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The surrounding Ōzono archaeological site is recognized as a significant settlement from the Kofun period, featuring a large number of pillar-supported buildings. Ōzono Kofun is identified as the burial site of a local chief. In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4. File:大園古墳出土 埴輪 鶏.JPG|Haniwa (chicken)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|1=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}}.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Intypeational sign (Takaishi City, 1994) * {{Cite book|language=ja|year=1986|chapter=Ōzono Site|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} Osaka Volume|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Site (Heibonsha)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Matsumura, Takafumi|year=1989|chapter=Ōzono Kofun|title={{ill|Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Ōzono Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|WD=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Kohanezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|ja|信太貝吹山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Kaibukiyama Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大園古墳|version=91584117|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大園古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55535989}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaishi]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In northern Izumi, chief tombs are divided into two groups—northern and southern—and appear to alternate in their leadership roles, with Ōzono Kofun being part of this rotational system."Ōzono Site Excavation Report - Survey of the Kuzunohana District," Izumi City Board of Education, 2014, pp. 1–4.
